# Lecture Scenes

## Equation as balance
Aura says: Quick check-in. An equation is a math sentence where both sides match.
Visual: balance_scale
Student: Drag 5 to the empty side of the balance.
Success: Yes. Both sides match. That is the feeling of an equation.
Repair: Close. We want both sides to hold the same value.

## Equals sign
Aura says: Here is one small new piece. The equals sign means the left and right sides have the same value.
Visual: balance_scale
Student: Drag 5 to the empty side of the balance.
Success: Yes. Both sides match. That is the feeling of an equation.
Repair: Close. We want both sides to hold the same value.

## Left side / right side
Aura says: Here is one small new piece. Equations have two sides that we compare.
Visual: balance_scale
Student: Drag 5 to the empty side of the balance.
Success: Yes. Both sides match. That is the feeling of an equation.
Repair: Close. We want both sides to hold the same value.

## Variable
Aura says: Let us make this bridge clear. A variable like x is a number we do not know yet.
Visual: flip_tile
Student: Tap the tile to reveal the meaning.
Success: Good. x is a hidden number we are trying to understand.
Repair: Let us slow this part down and make it smaller.

## Substitution
Aura says: Here is one small new piece. Substitution means trying a value in place of x.
Visual: drag_match
Student: Match the idea to its meaning.
Success: Yes. That is the piece we needed.
Repair: Let us slow this part down and make it smaller.

## Squared
Aura says: Let us make this bridge clear. x squared means x multiplied by itself.
Visual: flip_tile
Student: Tap the tile to open it.
Success: Exactly. x² means x multiplied by itself.
Repair: Let us slow this part down and make it smaller.

## x² vs 2x
Aura says: Here is one small new piece. x² and 2x look similar but mean different things.
Visual: drag_match
Student: Match each tile to what it means.
Success: Yes. x² and 2x are different ideas.
Repair: Let us slow this part down and make it smaller.

## Quadratic form
Aura says: Let us make this bridge clear. A quadratic equation has an x² part.
Visual: math_tiles
Student: Tap the glowing x² tile.
Success: Yes. The x² part is the quadratic clue.
Repair: That part matters too. The special quadratic clue is the x² tile.

## Parabola
Aura says: Here is one small new piece. The graph of a quadratic is a smooth U-shaped curve.
Visual: parabola_graph
Student: Move the slider and watch the curve.
Success: Nice. The curve is the shape quadratics make.
Repair: Let us slow this part down and make it smaller.

## Roots
Aura says: Here is one small new piece. Roots are where the curve touches the x-axis.
Visual: root_dots
Student: Tap the glowing dots on the ground line.
Success: Yes. Those touch points are roots.
Repair: Let us slow this part down and make it smaller.

## Solving
Aura says: Here is one small new piece. Solving means finding the x-values that make the equation balance.
Visual: drag_match
Student: Match the idea to its meaning.
Success: Yes. That is the piece we needed.
Repair: Let us slow this part down and make it smaller.
